title: Velvety Argentinian Beef Tart
description: Velvety Argentinian Beef Tart is a cultured raw-friendly dish served solo featuring ready rolled shortcrust pastry seared beef tartare and delicate beef stock pearls that burst with flavor.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200C and place the ready rolled pastry on a parchment lined baking sheet.
Brush the pastry edges with olive oil, bake for 10 minutes until lightly golden, then cool to room temperature.
Combine minced beef, minced shallot, fresh lemon juice, Dijon mustard, Worcestershire sauce, and a pinch of salt in a bowl and mix until well blended.
Spoon the beef mixture onto the cooled pastry to fill each tart shape, then cover with a second pastry sheet and seal the edges with a fork.
Bake the assembled tarts for 8 minutes to set the pastry while keeping the beef raw, then remove and let cool.
Dissolve 1.5g sodium alginate in 200ml warm water using a blender until smooth and let rest for 15 minutes.
Drop 50ml beef stock into the alginate bath with a dropper to form 1-2mm spheres, allow them to set for 4 minutes.
Transfer the spheres to 100ml cold water containing 1.5g calcium lactate, wait 1 minute, then strain and rinse with fresh water.
Arrange each tart on a plate, top with 3-4 beef stock pearls for a velvety burst of flavor, and garnish with microgreens.
